name: Waimano Ridge Trail
tagline: A long, rewarding ridge hike through native forest to the Ko'olau summit — for hikers who want to earn their views.
parking: Free parking at the trailhead at the top of Waimano Home Road in Pacific Palisades
bestTime: Very early morning start — you need a full day for the summit push
talkStory: The trailhead sits above the former Waimano Home, a state institution that operated from 1919 to 1999 — the name Waimano means 'many waters' for the streams cutting through these ridges. Most locals do the first 3 miles to Waimano Pool, a freshwater swimming hole, as a moderate out-and-back. Beyond the pool, the 14-mile round trip through native 'ohi'a forest to the Ko'olau summit is one of the most pristine ecosystems accessible by trail on Oahu.
